hero.tagline_accent: We always show you where the information comes from.
hero.description: unsere.news is an open-source, community-driven platform for traceable research. The community votes on which questions get investigated. When a topic crosses the threshold, our AI produces a source analysis built strictly on primary sources. Every claim is traceable. Every source will be linked.

banner.text: Every report is AI-researched, editorially reviewed and traceable to primary sources.

transparent.title: Transparency
transparent.body: Every published sourced claim is traceable to a verifiable primary source — government PDFs, scientific papers, raw data, etc, everything accessible in a single click.

community.title: Community-Driven
community.body: The community decides which Topics get investigated — not what the answers are. No editorial gatekeeping. When a topic crosses the vote threshold, the research begins automatically.

open.title: Open Source
open.body: MIT licensed. Public repository from day one. The code, methodology, and funding are all open for inspection.

steps.b: Any member can submit a verifiable topic.
Topics with enough support get commissioned.
AI gathers primary sources; editors verify manually.
Article published with different point of views

trustStatement.heading: Why you shouldn't just trust everything
trustStatement.body: Also this platform makes decisions! Which sources to include, how to frame questions and which perspectives to surface. No platform is free from this. That's why unsere.news make every step visible — so you can follow along, question it, and disagree.

items.label: Check the sources
items.text: Every source analysis includes direct links to the original documents. Read them yourself.

items.label: Uncertainty is visible
items.text: Conflicting data or missing evidence is explicitly flagged — not hidden.

items.label: How this report was made
items.text: Every report includes a methodology section: sources used, sources excluded, data date, AI or human verification.

items.label: Objectivity filter
items.text: Emotional language in questions is reduced to make them verifiable — not a claim of neutrality.
